title: stream type: construct/draw/serialize/finite-eligibility
manager: A stream type — a lazily-produced sequence you can construct, draw from, and serialize — for randomness sources, input feeds, and generated sequences, with explicit rules for when a stream counts as finite and so keeps the machine checkable. Streams are how FSL handles "a source of values over time" without giving up replay: draw from the same seeded stream, get the same sequence, every time and everywhere.
cs: stream type with construct/draw/serialize; finite-eligibility rules for the checkable band; supplies the random-stream argument to rand (W6.52); §4.6.
